perturbation.original_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; but none of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; and each of them is jointly responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning all persons making the filing, regardless of individual knowledge of inaccuracies.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the responsibility clause. Originally, each party was only responsible for their own information unless they knew of inaccuracies of others. The modified text makes each party jointly responsible for the accuracy of all information, regardless of knowledge. This contradicts the original intent of limited liability, creating uncertainty about the extent of responsibility and potentially leading to disputes over who is liable for inaccuracies. This creates an in-text contradiction, as the parties are first said to not be responsible, then they become responsible, creating an ambiguous situation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder (the "Exchange Act") the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of Clarus IV-A, L.P., Clarus IV-B, L.P., Clarus IV-C, L.P., Clarus IV-D, L.P., Clarus IV GP, L.P., Blackstone Clarus GP L.P., Blackstone Clarus GP L.L.C., Blackstone Holdings II L.P., Blackstone Holdings I/II GP L.L.C., The Blackstone Group Inc., Blackstone Group Management L.L.C. and Stephen A. Schwarzman, on behalf of each of them of any filing required by such party under Section 13 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with respect to securities of Galera Therapeutics, Inc., a Delaware corporation, and further agree to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this Agreement as an exhibit thereto. Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; and each of them is jointly responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning all persons making the filing, regardless of individual knowledge of inaccuracies. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent by making each party jointly responsible for all information, regardless of individual knowledge. This conflicts with the original text, where each party was responsible only for their own information unless they had knowledge of inaccuracies of others. This is a strong in-text contradiction because it fundamentally alters the liability and responsibility framework, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes. The contradicted text includes the changed text itself, as the alteration creates a direct inconsistency within the agreement regarding liability for the accuracy of information.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto. However, any revocation shall be effective immediately for all parties regardless of notification.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the terms of revocation. Originally, revocation was only effective for the revoking party after written notification. The modified text states that any revocation is effective immediately for *all* parties, regardless of notification. This contradicts the original intent of the agreement's enforceability and creates uncertainty about its duration and application. Because, it first states that the agreement will remain in effect until a party revokes it, however any revocation is effective immediately for all parties regardless of notification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder (the "Exchange Act") the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of Clarus IV-A, L.P., Clarus IV-B, L.P., Clarus IV-C, L.P., Clarus IV-D, L.P., Clarus IV GP, L.P., Blackstone Clarus GP L.P., Blackstone Clarus GP L.L.C., Blackstone Holdings II L.P., Blackstone Holdings I/II GP L.L.C., The Blackstone Group Inc., Blackstone Group Management L.L.C. and Stephen A. Schwarzman, on behalf of each of them of any filing required by such party under Section 13 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with respect to securities of Galera Therapeutics, Inc., a Delaware corporation, and further agree to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this Agreement as an exhibit thereto. Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; but none of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto. However, any revocation shall be effective immediately for all parties regardless of notification. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement remains in effect until revoked by a party with signed written notice to other parties, and revocation is only effective for the revoking party. The changed text modifies this by stating that any revocation is effective immediately for all parties, regardless of notification. This directly contradicts the initial condition that the agreement remains in effect until proper notification, as well as the limitation of revocation to only the revoking party. The contradicted text contains the modified revocation clause, which directly creates an in-text contradiction with the first sentence regarding the agreement remaining in effect until written revocation.
